- Path 2.4: Among the authors identified as having prior papers, select the one whose earliest paper is the first chronologically (if multiple) – or the unique author with prior work, as the task implies.

- Path 2.5: For the selected author, locate the original publication of their earliest paper (e.g., via DOI or PDF) to confirm the exact title formatting (spacing, punctuation, capitalization).

New task:
Of the authors (First M. Last) that worked on the paper "Pie Menus or Linear Menus, Which Is Better?" in 2015, what was the title of the first paper authored by the one that had authored prior papers?

————Memory System Guidance————
- Verify the exact author list from the primary source (e.g., ACM Digital Library) for the 2015 paper, noting full names and order.  
- Use a reliable bibliographic database (e.g., DBLP) to retrieve each author’s publication history sorted by year; identify which author(s) have papers prior to 2015.  
- Locate that author’s earliest published paper, confirm its title from the original source (e.g., DOI page or PDF), and return it exactly as formatted (spacing, punctuation).
————End Memory————
Now, begin your planning analysis for this task!

[PLAN]:
## Goal 1: Retrieve the exact author list for the 2015 paper "Pie Menus or Linear Menus, Which Is Better?"  
- Path 1.1: Search ACM Digital Library for the paper by title and year, crawl the official paper page, and extract the full author names in the order they appear.  
  - Success: Author list is obtained from the primary source (ACM DL) with full names and correct order.  
- Path 1.2: Use DBLP to locate the same paper entry, extract the author list, and cross‑verify with the ACM DL results for consistency.  
  - Success: Author list is confirmed by two independent reliable sources.  
- Path 1.3: Search Google Scholar for the paper; if found, compare the displayed authors with those from the previous sources to ensure completeness.  
  - Success: Author list is validated across three sources, any discrepancies resolved.  

## Goal 2: For each author identified in Goal 1, determine which had authored papers before 2015 and obtain the title of that author’s earliest published paper.  
- Path 2.1: For author X, access their DBLP profile page, retrieve the full list of publications sorted by year, note the earliest publication year < 2015, and record the title of that first paper.  
  - Success: Author X’s earliest pre‑2015 paper title is captured from DBLP.  
- Path 2.2: For author Y, repeat the same DBLP‑based procedure.  
  - Success: Author Y’s prior publication status and earliest title are obtained.  
- Path 2.3: For author Z (and any further authors), repeat the same procedure.  
  - Success: All authors’ prior‑paper statuses and earliest titles (if any) are collected in parallel.  
- Path 2.4: Among the authors identified as having prior papers, select the one whose earliest paper is the first chronologically (if multiple) – or the unique author with prior work, as the task implies.  
  - Success: The specific target author is determined.  
- Path 2.5: For the selected author, locate the original publication of their earliest paper (e.g., via DOI or PDF) to confirm the exact title formatting (spacing, punctuation, capitalization).  
  - Success: The precise title, as it appears in the original source, is obtained.  

**Note:** Goal 2 depends on the output of Goal 1 (the author list) before its paths can begin execution. Within Goal 2, Paths 2.1–2.3 can run in parallel for each author, while Paths 2.4 and 2.5 are sequential after those results.

————Memory System Guidance————
Always verify exact wording from primary sources (PDFs) rather than relying on secondary summaries. Use multiple parallel queries and cross-referencing to increase robustness.
————End Memory————
